final.note: Average coverage across 9 Phase 206 target files

improvement.note: Negative because Phase 206 files have lower average coverage than baseline

target_vs_actual.explanation: Plan assumed 80% would be achieved by testing 10 files. Actual average coverage across 9 files is 56.79%. Some files have very low coverage (workflow_engine: 10%, episode_segmentation: 15%, byok_handler: 25%).

summary.note: 3 collection errors when running full test suite (memory service tests)

key_achievements: Created 298 comprehensive tests across 10 core files
4 files achieved 75%+ coverage: governance_cache (93.1%), context_resolver (99.15%), cognitive_tier (90%), workflow_template (83.41%)
Validated test infrastructure for Phase 207 coverage expansion
Documented coverage patterns and challenges for complex modules
challenges_encountered: 3 files with very low coverage: workflow_engine (10.13%), episode_segmentation (15.38%), byok_handler (25.22%)
Collection errors in memory service tests when running full test suite
governance_cache.py not included in final coverage run (module path issue)
Complex async modules (workflow_engine, episode_segmentation) difficult to test comprehensively
Overall backend coverage remained at 74.6% (did not achieve 80% target)
lessons_learned: Coverage expansion strategy requires selecting files with achievable coverage targets
Large complex modules (>1000 lines) like workflow_engine are difficult to cover >50%
Async service testing requires significant mock setup and careful test design
Collection stability is challenging when integrating new tests into existing suite
File-level quality (75%+) more important than chasing overall percentage
Module selection is critical: some modules (governance) test well, others (workflow) don't
recommendations_for_phase_207: Focus on modules with achievable 75%+ coverage (avoid >1000 line files)
Improve low-coverage modules from Phase 206: workflow_engine, episode_segmentation, byok_handler
Fix collection errors in memory service tests before expanding
Consider branch coverage improvement in addition to line coverage
Target modules with high testability (API routes, tools, simpler services)
Set realistic targets: 60-70% average coverage may be more achievable than 80%
deviations_from_plan: Plan assumed 80% coverage achieved, actual is 56.79% (23.21pp gap)
Plan expected 10 files covered, actual is 9 (missing governance_cache)
Plan expected zero collection errors, actual is 3 (memory service tests)
Plan expected file-level quality (75%+), actual is 4/9 files (44%)
Overall backend coverage did not increase from 74.69% baseline
next_steps: Phase 207: Focus on high-testability modules to achieve realistic coverage gains
Improve test coverage for 3 low-coverage Phase 206 files
Fix collection errors to ensure test stability
Consider alternative coverage metrics (branch coverage, critical path coverage)
Document modules that are not cost-effective to test comprehensively
